steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a cause-and-effect: the 1903 Wright Flyer was bulky and not aerodynamic; modern planes have sleek, streamlined bodies. The blank introduces the logical result of that sleek design. A causal transition is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Lastly,: Signals a final item in a list, not a cause-and-effect relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — On the contrary,: Signals a contradiction. The third sentence does not contradict the second — it follows from it. ✗
  • Choice C — As a result,: Directly signals that the greater aerodynamics is a consequence of the sleek, streamlined bodies described in the previous sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — Regardless,: Signals that the third sentence holds true despite the second — the opposite of the intended meaning. ✗
That is the correct answer.
